threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think crisps are a snack, not a sandwich filler. And don’t get me started on your confusion over our real bacon rolls.
Caught a night bus home last week, and a guy started rapping about how the 333 is the only way to escape a bad date. Honest vibe, full of laughs.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was really home when my Berliner neighbor insisted on teaching me how to properly roll a Kebab while we argued over the best place in town.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst from a stand while a street musician serenades you with a bagpipe cover of Beethoven. Pure Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate a BART delay—while newcomers freak out, we just pull out a snack and wait.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The guy playing the accordion at 24th and Mission made the bus wait, but everyone smiled. Only in San Francisco do we turn delays into street performances.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the bars, but the real hidden gems are the tiny shops that sell fresh pa amb tomàquet every morning.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still gawking at the street art instead of ducking into that tapas bar on every corner. A dead giveaway.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a good idea to bring a suitcase to Dalston Junction instead of a backpack. That hotel vibe won't fly here.
Last night on the N37, a guy pulled out a saxophone and played ‘Careless Whisper’ while we all sat in silence. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Listening to the morning buskers on Karl-Marx-Strasse, one strumming a ukulele while a toddler twirled, made me realize this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a flea market where a DJ spins vinyl while locals haggle over retro furniture. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they casually mention the BART delays during Friday dinner plans like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling on the 19 Polk this morning. He had a sign saying 'life’s a circus'—and honestly, it felt like it for everyone onboard.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' means sharing plates. Locals actually prefer 'platillos'—smaller, solo servings, so you can savor each one.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, while the locals glide right through.
